dailyNews.title: 'Nobody knows what happened to him': Trump on reports regarding Mojtaba Khamenei's health
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday addressed the rumours regarding the health of Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, saying that nobody knows what happened to him. His remarks came as several reports speculated that the leader is either dead or severely injured.

dailyNews.title: Melania Trump attends her own global summit for just 7 minutes on day one; Israeli PM's wife among attendees
dailyNews.description: Melania arrived at 10:01 am, addressed attendees, and departed at 10:08 am shortly after completing her speech.

dailyNews.title: EXCLUSIVE: Cameras capture truckers unable to read road signs, answer basic questions during Florida crackdown
dailyNews.description: Troopers say up to half of truckers at some Florida weigh stations fail English proficiency tests as the Trump administration ramps up Department of Transportation enforcement.

dailyNews.title: Conservatives accuse Jack Smith of improper ties with judges in Trump cases after new document dump
dailyNews.description: Sen. Chuck Grassley released documents critics allege show Jack Smith's team improperly coordinated with judges Beryl Howell and James Boasberg in Trump probes.

dailyNews.title: Minnesota sues Trump admin to access evidence in federal shootings, including Alex Pretti, Renee Good cases
dailyNews.description: Minnesota AG Keith Ellison and local officials are suing the Trump administration, naming Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem in a battle over "Metro Surge" shooting evidence.

dailyNews.title: Why President Trump Has a Big Oil Problem
dailyNews.description: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger looks at President Trump’s trouble handling retaliatory attacks by Iran that have largely choked off the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
dailyNews.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Threat to ‘Obliterate’ Iran’s Power Stations Could Constitute a War Crime, Rights Experts Say
dailyNews.description: Intentionally targeting the country’s energy infrastructure could constitute a war crime under international law.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
dailyNews.description: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.

dailyNews.title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
dailyNews.description: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

dailyNews.title: Costa Rica Agrees to Take Migrants Deported by the Trump Administration
dailyNews.description: The agreement is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept people who have been detained in the United States.

dailyNews.title: N.C. Senator Phil Berger Officially Just Lost to Sam Page
dailyNews.description: Phil Berger had money, power and an endorsement from President Trump. But his critics had a long list of resentments and, on Tuesday, he lost by a mere 23 votes.
